sources.title: Murder at the Baskervilles
sources.description: Title: Murder at the Baskervilles Summary: Sherlock Holmes takes a vacation and visits his old friend Sir Henry Baskerville. His vacation ends when he suddenly finds himself in the middle of a double-murder mystery. Now he's got to ... Directed by: Thomas Bentley Actors: Arthur Wontner, Ian Fleming, Lyn Harding Production Company: Julius Hagen Productions Release Date: 15 January 1941 (USA) Aspect Ratio: 1.37 : 1 Sherlock Holmes takes a vacation and visits his old friend Sir Henry Baskerville. His vacation ends when he suddenly finds himself in the middle of a double-murder mystery. Now he's got to find Professor Moriarty and the horse Silver Blaze before the great cup final horse race.

sources.title: Murder At The Baskervilles
sources.description: From IMDb : Sherlock Holmes takes a vacation and visits his old friend Sir Henry Baskerville. His vacation ends when he suddenly finds himself in the middle of a double-murder mystery. Now he's got to find Professor Moriarty and the horse Silver Blaze before the great cup final horse race.

sources.title: Murder At The Baskervilles ( 1937) Sherlock Holmes Crime & Mystery
sources.description: Returning to Baskerville Hall, brilliant detective Sherlock Holmes (Arthur Wontner) envisions a relaxing holiday with his dear friend Sir Henry Baskerville (Lawrence Grossmith). Soon, however, Holmes and his intrepid assistant, Dr. Watson (Ian Fleming), become ensnared in a plot to fix a prestigious  race in which Baskerville's horse, Silver Blaze, is the favorite. When two people are found dead as a result of the gambling plot, suspicion falls on Holmes' bitter rival, Moriarty (Lyn Harding).

sources.title: Murder at the Baskervilles (1937 Myestery) Haunted Hound? Sherlock Holmes Hunts Truth!
sources.description:
Sherlock Holmes sprints past red herrings following the prized steed Silver Blaze's vanishing act, his mental gymnastics untangling fiendish turf schemes while Watson stands guard. Their swift pursuit exposes the schemer and derails the nefarious plans, proving even the fastest hooves can't outrun a determined detective.

Director: Thomas Bentley
Writers: Arthur Conan Doyle, Arthur Macrae, H. Fowler Mear

Original title: Silver Blaze (1937)

Cast:
Arthur Wontner - Sherlock Holmes
Ian Fleming - Dr. Watson
Lyn Harding - Professor Moriarty
John Turnbull - Inspector Lestrade
Robert Horton - Col. Ross
Lawrence Grossmith - Sir Henry Baskerville
Judy Gunn - Diana Baskerville
Arthur Macrae - Jack Trevor
Arthur Goullet - Col. Sebastian Moran
Martin Walker - James Straker
Eve Gray - Mrs. Mary Straker
Gilbert Davis - Miles Stanford
Minnie Rayner - Mrs. Hudson
D. J. Williams - Silas Brown
Ralph Truman - Bert Prince
Ronald Shiner - Simpson the Stable Boy / Jockey (uncredited)
Genres: Crime, Mystery
